gaps.title: Confidence score race condition
gaps.breaks: Simultaneous updates to confidence scores create inconsistent state
gaps.scenario: Two agents process the same ticket concurrently, one updates relevance score to 0.8, the other updates completeness to 0.9, but the final stored score becomes corrupted or only one value persists
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent updates to confidence scores for the same ticket. Create two worker processes that simultaneously update different dimensions (relevance and completeness) of the same ticket's confidence score. Verify the final stored score contains both updated values and no data corruption.

gaps.title: Guardrail bypass via content chunking
gaps.breaks: Large content bypasses PII detection when split into chunks
gaps.scenario: User uploads a document with PII information, but when it's processed in chunks, each chunk individually passes PII detection, allowing sensitive data to slip through the guardrails
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates a document containing PII information (SSN, email, phone) and tests the guardrail pipeline with various chunk sizes. Verify that PII detection fails when the PII is split across chunks but the content is semantically connected.

gaps.title: False negative in hallucination detection
gaps.scenario: Agent generates a response that includes completely fabricated product specifications, but the hallucination detection fails because it uses similar language patterns to accurate information
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates a response with deliberately fabricated information mixed with accurate facts. Test the hallucination detection guardrail with various types of false information (product specs, dates, statistics) and measure false negative rates.

gaps.title: Tenant isolation in guardrail results
gaps.breaks: Guardrail configuration from one tenant affects another
gaps.scenario: Tenant A has strict content policies, Tenant B has lenient ones, but when processing requests, the wrong tenant's policy configuration is applied, causing false positives/negatives
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two tenants with different guardrail policy configurations. Process identical content through both tenants' guardrail pipelines and verify that the results differ according to each tenant's specific configuration, with no cross-contamination.

gaps.title: Confidence threshold cascade failure
gaps.breaks: Low confidence score triggers blocking but doesn't provide fallback
gaps.scenario: Agent generates a response with low confidence score (0.6), triggering the confidence-based blocking guardrail, but no alternative response is provided, leaving the customer without any answer
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates various confidence scores (0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9) and verifies the system behavior when each threshold is crossed. Ensure that when blocking occurs, appropriate fallback responses are provided and logged.

gaps.title: Guardrail pipeline silent failure
gaps.breaks: One guardrail failure stops processing without alerting
gaps.scenario: The PII detection guardrail fails unexpectedly, causing the entire guardrail pipeline to halt silently, allowing all subsequent content to bypass all other guardrails
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a failure in one guardrail component (PII detection) and verifies that the pipeline either continues with remaining guardrails or fails gracefully with proper alerting, rather than silently allowing content to pass through unchecked.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 6

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Confidence score race condition
What breaks: Simultaneous updates to confidence scores create inconsistent state
Real scenario: Two agents process the same ticket concurrently, one updates relevance score to 0.8, the other updates completeness to 0.9, but the final stored score becomes corrupted or only one value persists
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent updates to confidence scores for the same ticket. Create two worker processes that simultaneously update different dimensions (relevance and completeness) of the same ticket's confidence score. Verify the final stored score contains both updated values and no data corruption.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Guardrail bypass via content chunking
What breaks: Large content bypasses PII detection when split into chunks
Real scenario: User uploads a document with PII information, but when it's processed in chunks, each chunk individually passes PII detection, allowing sensitive data to slip through the guardrails
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates a document containing PII information (SSN, email, phone) and tests the guardrail pipeline with various chunk sizes. Verify that PII detection fails when the PII is split across chunks but the content is semantically connected.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: False negative in hallucination detection
What breaks: Fabricated information passes as accurate
Real scenario: Agent generates a response that includes completely fabricated product specifications, but the hallucination detection fails because it uses similar language patterns to accurate information
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates a response with deliberately fabricated information mixed with accurate facts. Test the hallucination detection guardrail with various types of false information (product specs, dates, statistics) and measure false negative rates.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Tenant isolation in guardrail results
What breaks: Guardrail configuration from one tenant affects another
Real scenario: Tenant A has strict content policies, Tenant B has lenient ones, but when processing requests, the wrong tenant's policy configuration is applied, causing false positives/negatives
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two tenants with different guardrail policy configurations. Process identical content through both tenants' guardrail pipelines and verify that the results differ according to each tenant's specific configuration, with no cross-contamination.

GAP 5
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Confidence threshold cascade failure
What breaks: Low confidence score triggers blocking but doesn't provide fallback
Real scenario: Agent generates a response with low confidence score (0.6), triggering the confidence-based blocking guardrail, but no alternative response is provided, leaving the customer without any answer
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates various confidence scores (0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9) and verifies the system behavior when each threshold is crossed. Ensure that when blocking occurs, appropriate fallback responses are provided and logged.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Guardrail pipeline silent failure
What breaks: One guardrail failure stops processing without alerting
Real scenario: The PII detection guardrail fails unexpectedly, causing the entire guardrail pipeline to halt silently, allowing all subsequent content to bypass all other guardrails
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a failure in one guardrail component (PII detection) and verifies that the pipeline either continues with remaining guardrails or fails gracefully with proper alerting, rather than silently allowing content to pass through unchecked.
